seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: composite: keep the min-length connectivity goal but (a) restrict the output to a per-location degree-capped network (caps in {2,3,4}), and (b) generalize the input so only a marked subset of locations (light poles) must be connected while the rest (junction cabinets) are optional Steiner points -- turning the polynomial Euclidean minimum spanning tree into the NP-hard degree-constrained Steiner tree.
title: Luminet: Degree-Capped Smart-City Lighting Backhaul
strategies: pole-chain baseline: wire the light poles in input index order into a single path (always feasible since every pole cap >= 2, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour pole path: start at a pole and repeatedly hop to the closest unvisited pole, ignoring junction cabinets -- a short degree-2 chain

degree-constrained Steiner tree: run Kruskal/Prim with port-cap limits over ALL locations (poles + cabinets), repair any leftover components via spare-capacity nodes, then prune junction-cabinet leaves so cabinets are kept only where they shorten routing
local search: start from a degree-constrained Steiner tree and apply length-reducing edge swaps / reroutes through cabinets that respect the caps
